title: Multimedia Content Creator
description: **About the Role** ------------------ At Thérapie Clinic, we’ve been enhancing confidence and empowering lives for over 20 years. With more than 85 clinics across the UK, Ireland, and the USA, we’re proud to be Europe’s No.1 provider of Aesthetic Treatments. Our mission is simple: to make advanced, effective treatments accessible to everyone. Enjoy a fulfilling and rewarding career with opportunities for growth, development, and long\-term success. Join us and Do Your Best Work at Thérapie! As we continue to scale rapidly, we are looking for a talented and experienced **Multimedia Content Creator** to join us. This role offers the opportunity to shape the future of our workforce. The role of **Multimedia Content Creator** will join the Marketing Team based in Dublin. As someone who lives and breathes creativity, understands social media trends, and has the skills to bring ideas to life through engaging video and photography content, you’ll be responsible for producing scroll\-stopping content, working closely with our design and performance teams to deliver high\-impact creative including Long format Video, Short Format Reels and Photography assets for Thérapie Clinic. **Key Responsibilities** ------------------------ * Plan, shoot, and edit video and photo content tailored for social media platforms and our websites. * Long\-form content that tells a deeper story. For example: Explaining complex treatment services, Immersive Brand Storytelling \& Testimonials, Educational \& Authority\-Building Content, Comprehensive Product Education \& Conversion or guiding viewers toward a decision. * Short Format Reels/videos: Hook\-led, visually engaging creative videos for social media. Knowledge and experience with social media trends and visual hooks vital. Must speak the language of reels. Edit and repurpose longer videos into shorter clips * Work closely and proactively with design and performance teams to align content with campaign goals, brand standards, and in\-clinic execution. * Video editing/ photo retouching * Collaborate with the design team to deliver polished, on\-brand and elevated UGC feel content. * Take ownership of content projects from concept to delivery * Proactively pitch new ideas and content formats * Create content that drives engagement, reach, and conversions across social platforms, delivering content aligned with performance marketing goals. * Stay up\-to\-date with trends and techniques in video and photography for social media. * Travel to various locations across Ireland and the UK. * Full, clean driving licence preferred **Required Qualifications and Skills:** * Proven experience in video production (filming, lighting, audio capture). * Expertise in **Adobe Premiere Pro** or **Final Cut Pro**. * Proficiency in **CapCut** for mobile and social media editing. * Experience in both short\-form (Reels/TikTok) and long\-form video editing. * Skilled in storytelling and storyboarding techniques. * Experience in video color grading. * Strong photography skills, including camera handling and lighting. * Basic photo editing/retouching proficiency in **Adobe Creative Suite** (Photoshop/Lightroom). **Preferred Qualifications (Nice to Haves)** * Experience with **Adobe After Effects** for motion graphics. * Experience creating graphics in **Canva**. **Qualifications and Skills** ----------------------------- * Ideal for a recent graduate with a strong portfolio * 1–3 years’ experience (including freelance, internships, or personal projects)os (experience with tools like Adobe Premiere Pro, Final Cut Pro, or similar is a plus). * Strong understanding of short\-form, social\-first content (Reels, TikTok, Shorts) * Proven experience in storyboarding, scripting, filming, and editing video content. * Demonstrable experience in photography. * Comfortable shooting both high\-end and mobile\-first content * Brings creativity, energy, and adaptability to every project. * Is highly organised and comfortable working on multiple projects at once. * What matters most is your skill, eye for detail, and passion for content creation. **Over to the best part, the Perks!** ------------------------------------- * #### **Uncapped Earnings** With our uncapped commission structure, the sky's the limit! * #### **Treat Yourself** Enjoy free laser hair removal, Free EmSella and generous discounts on our skin, AWI and body treatments – because our skin maestros deserve the best. * #### **Share the Love** Avail spouse and family discounts, spreading the joy of Thérapie. * #### **Sisterly Love** 10% off Thérapie Fertility \& Optilase treatments – because family helps family. * #### **Thérapie Fertility** We offer one free treatment cycle of IVF/ICSI/RIVF to all team members after they have been with us for 2 years. * #### **Optilase Eye Clinic** We offer free laser eye surgery to all team members after they have been with us for 2 years. * #### **Skin Royalty** Discounts on leading skin brands \& treatments (Murad, Skin Theory). * #### **Career Pathways** Embark on a journey of career progression \& management training – we believe in promoting from within! * #### **Knowledge Oasis** Immerse yourself in a full week of paid training in our academy, where knowledge is power. * #### **Big Birthday Bash Leave** Enjoy an extra day off on your landmark birthdays to celebrate in style! * #### **Longevity Bonus** Earn extra annual leave days after 2 years of dedicated service. * #### **Employee Assistance Scheme** Access confidential support services for personal and professional well\-being. **Application Process** ----------------------- STEP 1 **Explore Opportunities****:** Start by finding the role that excites you. STEP 2 **Submit Your Application****:** Apply by uploading your CV. STEP 3 **Application Review****:** Our Talent Acquisition Partners will carefully review your application. STEP 4 **Initial Contact****:** If shortlisted, our Talent Acquisition Team will reach out to schedule an interview. STEP 5 **Interview Stage****:** Complete the interview process with our team. STEP 6 **Offer \& Onboarding****:** If successful, you'll receive your offer along with onboarding details. STEP 7 **Begin Your Journey****:** Attend our state\-of\-the\-art training facility to kickstart your career with us.

title: Operations Risk & Resilience Placement - 2026 Work Placement
description: **Shape a future with purpose at Arup in Dublin and Cork.** Arup’s purpose, shared values and collaborative approach has set us apart for over 75 years, guiding how we shape a better world. The Operations Consulting Team Supports Clients Across The Full Asset Lifecycle—from Acquisition And Planning Through To Operation And Disposal—by Addressing The Interdependencies Between People, Processes, Systems, And Assets. The Discipline Delivers a Broad And Integrated Set Of Services, Including * Operational Strategy \& Transformation: Helping clients align operational execution with strategic goals, including digital transformation initiatives and process improvement * Governance, Risk \& Compliance (GRC): Support the delivery of effective compliance and risk management programmes to identify, manage and mitigate business and digital related risk * Operational Readiness \& Performance Improvement: Ensuring systems, teams, and processes are prepared for launch or transition, and continuously optimised for performance * Digital Enablement: Leveraging tools like BIM, Digital Twins, and analytics platforms to improve operational visibility and decision\-making **The Opportunity** Arup Ireland are seeking talented students to join our Cork or Dublin team. We look for students who are ready to jump into the professional world; diverse individuals with inquisitive minds and a desire to challenge. You will work alongside skilled professionals and contribute to the success of real projects. You will also enjoy learning sessions with technical experts within your team, working on local projects and all kinds of social events. You will be working with a team of Operations Consulting Services professionals from various offices. The Operations Consulting team embodies Arup’s Total Design philosophy by integrating people, processes, technology, and physical assets into cohesive operational strategies that are embedded from concept through to implementation. The team works with a variety of public sector organisations, including Transport Infrastructure Ireland. With reference to our Risk \& Resilience service within Operations Consulting, it exemplifies Arup’s Total Design by embedding resilient governance frameworks, proactive risk intelligence, and compliance assurance into the operational phase of projects—ensuring that systems, processes, and people are holistically integrated. This is an office\-based role, with successful candidates expected to work 5 days per week in the Arup office in Cork or Dublin. **As a work placement student in the Operations team at Arup, you will:** * Support turning technical recommendations into operational plans and project deliverables with defined sustainability outcomes for our clients * Practice creative applications of technology to develop design solutions * Develop communication skills, including report writing and visual representations * Attend and participate in meetings – listen, ask questions * Be involved in discussions with team members to gain knowledge \& experience * Undertake analytical research to develop, document, present concepts and design ideas * Review project documentation, applicable codes and standards to assist with preparation of reports, presentations, drawings and/or sketches * Give structure to ideas – lots of “taking a first pass” at spreadsheets, reports and analyses for discussion later rather than instruction **Does this role inspire you?** We're Looking For Students Who Are Currently Studying For a Relevant Bachelor’s Degree Level Or Be Currently Undertaking a Post\-graduate Level Qualification In a Relevant Discipline, Including, But Not Limited To * Governance \& Compliance Management * Business in Operations Management * Management (Operations \& Supply Chain) * Supply Chain Operations * Business Information Systems * Computer Systems Engineering * Cybersecurity **We Also Value** * Experience of producing technical presentations and reports, knowledge sharing with peers * History of academic research, technical presentations, and technical report writing * Experience with any of the following: GRC software solutions, Cybersecurity solutions, Microsoft Visio, PowerPoint, or Project, or analysis packages * Knowledge of ISO Assurance Frameworks such as 27001 or 55001 and Data Privacy regulations * Evidence of teamwork and leadership in academic or extracurricular field * The ability to prioritise effectively, balance assigned work and deliver to deadlines **Why Arup?** At Arup, we believe in investing in our people and supporting their growth from day one. As a trust\-owned company, we're driven by our purpose and values, creating a collaborative and rewarding environment for our employees. **Benefits That Work For You** * Professional Growth \& Development: Kickstart your career with our structured program, benefit from continuous learning opportunities, and receive guidance from experienced mentors. * Wellbeing and Culture: At Arup we are committed to fostering a healthy working culture by offering a range of health and wellbeing benefits, including health insurance, flexible working schemes, wellness programmes and an Employee Assistance Programme. * Equality, Diversity and Inclusion: We strive to embed inclusion in our way of working, our interactions with one another and the projects we deliver. We are committed to creating an inclusive working environment for talented people of any background, based on merit, fairness and respect. At Arup, you belong to an extraordinary collective where individuality is encouraged. Our strength comes from how we respect, share, and connect our diverse experiences, perspectives, and ideas. You'll have the opportunity to do socially useful work that has meaning – to Arup, to your career, and to the clients and communities we serve. We welcome applications from talented individuals of all backgrounds, regardless of age (within legal limits), gender identity or expression, disability, ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, or any other protected characteristic. We are committed to making our recruitment process and workplaces accessible. If you need any assistance or adjustments, please visit our Candidate Support page for contact information and assistance. We're happy to help. **Please note this role is only suitable for those who are in full time education and are looking for a placement for the summer months or for those who are required to complete a placement as part of their course requirements.** **If you have recently graduated, please apply to one of our '2026 Graduate Programme' roles.** \#IREIntern **Do you want to join us in shaping a better world?**

title: Plasterer
description: Job Title: Plasterer (Full\-Time) Location: Meath / Dublin / Kildare (Site\-Based) Job Type: Full\-Time, Permanent Salary: Competitive – Based on Experience Job Description: We are currently seeking a reliable and hardworking plasterer to join a 2:1 gang for ongoing full\-time work. The role involves machine plastering on active construction sites across Meath, Dublin, and Kildare. Machine plastering experience is an advantage but not essential, as training can be provided for the right candidate. Key Responsibilities: * Carry out plastering work on\-site to a high standard * Work efficiently as part of a 2:1 gang * Operate or assist with plastering machines * Follow all site health and safety regulations Requirements: * Valid Safe Pass * Manual Handling Certificate * Own transport (essential due to site locations) * Strong work ethic and reliability * Previous plastering experience preferred What We Offer: * Full\-time, ongoing work * Competitive pay (depending on experience) * Opportunity to gain machine plastering experience How to Apply: Apply directly through Indeed or contact us for more information Job Type: Permanent Pay: €220\.00\-€250\.00 per day Work Location: On the road

title: QC Analyst - Alexion
description: **QC Biochemistry Analyst** **Location:** Dublin, Ireland **Reports to:** QC Biochemistry Supervisor Competitive salary and bonus **This Is What You Will Do** The individual in this position should have a working knowledge of the cGMP QC laboratory environment and laboratory equipment associated with biopharmaceutical analysis. The position requires the ability to understand and follow written SOPs, to accurately and legibly record work in real time, and pay close attention to detail. The individual must be able to perform routine tasks with a minimum of supervision. **You Will Be Responsible For** * Perform all lab functions in compliance with cGMP * Follow written procedures for the following tests, as trained and qualified: + Gel electrophoresis (SDS\-PAGE, IEF) + ELISA and plate based assays (potency and residual assays such as HCP and ProA) * Prioritisation of samples for testing (release, stability and in\-process testing) * Reviewing of laboratory data, including logbooks and test executions. * Recognize and report aberrant test results and sample conditions. * Training of new analysts in specific assays. * Drive continuous improvement initiatives within the QC department. * Complete execution of investigations in a timely manner, ensuring they are updated in real time. * Provide lab support including reagent preparation, cleaning, and routine equipment maintenance. * Ensure training is current for all job functions performed. * Order, stock and receive laboratory supplies. * Complete all required documentation legibly and accurately. **You Will Need To Have** * BSc in Chemistry or a biological science, with 1\-4 years’ experience, or equivalent combination of education and experience. **We Would Prefer For You To Have** * A working knowledge of the cGMP QC laboratory environment, and equipment associated with testing of biopharmaceutical products * Ability to follow written procedures with close attention to detail * Ability to function with minimal supervision for routine job duties. * A working knowledge of gel electrophoresis and ELISA assays **Date Posted** 17\-Apr\-2026 **Closing Date** 22\-Apr\-2026 Our mission is to build an inclusive and equitable environment. We want people to feel they belong at AstraZeneca and Alexion, starting with our recruitment process. We welcome and consider applications from all qualified candidates, regardless of characteristics. We offer reasonable adjustments/accommodations to help all candidates to perform at their best. If you have a need for any adjustments/accommodations, please complete the section in the application form.

title: Quantity Surveyor - Transport
description: **Location/s:** Dublin, IE **Recruiter contact:** William Bates Mott MacDonald is a global engineering, management, and development consultancy with over 20,000 employees across more than 50 countries and 140\+ offices. We work across incredible global industries, delivering exciting work that is defining our future and making an important societal impact in the communities we serve. Our people power our performance – we succeed when they do. With countless opportunities to collaborate, learn, and grow, the possibilities for excellence are as varied as every individual. Whether you want to grow as a subject matter expert or broaden your experience with roles across our international community, you’re surrounded by global specialists who want to combine their expertise and champion you to be your best. As a proudly employee\-owned business, we benefit our clients, our communities, and each other, investing in creating the right space for everyone to feel empowered, included, and valued. Whatever your ambition, Mott MacDonald is where people come to be **brilliant.** **About The Division** The advisory and programme delivery divisiondelivers services to projects ranging in scale and complexity from a few million pounds to £20bn through the technical disciplines of land advisory, programme management, project management, project controls, scheduling, cost management, estimating, infrastructure finance, management consulting, digital consulting, education and healthcare management. Our transportation portfolio is primarily a UK based business working in four key sectors of rail, highways, maritime and aviation. We deliver programme, project and commercial management services to clients in mainland Europe, Africa, Middle East, India and Australasia while supporting other parts of the transportation business across the globe. What is our vision? Our vision, with your help, is to deliver major transportation projects as part of a global business and to establish our wide\-ranging consultancy as credible and preferred for our existing and new clients. **About The Team** Our transportation team is expanding, and we have multiple exciting opportunities for project and commercial managers. Our core team consists of professionally qualified and experienced Project/Programme Managers, Planners, Commercial Managers, and Quantity Surveyors. We lead and deliver a broad range of exciting transportation projects and programmes through our integrated, full service, Project, Programme, and Commercial Management (PPCM). We deliver a one Mott MacDonald solution by collaborating closely with our clients in the transportation sector and leveraging Mott MacDonald’s technical excellence in engineering, development, and management services. We are seeking driven professionals with the right attitude to integrate both internally and externally, grow our team, represent Mott MacDonald, and to deliver excellent service to our clients. We operate in Aviation, Highways, Rail and Maritime. Our vision is to deliver major transportation projects as part of a global business and to establish our wide\-ranging consultancy as ‘credible’ and ‘preferred’ for our existing and new clients. **Overview Of The Role** As Quantity Surveyor you will be responsible for the commercial management of multiple projects and workstreams on major highways projects. **Key Responsibilities And Duties Include** * Responsible for commercially managing a project or multiple projects, including: + Budget management including month end reporting (actuals, monthly variances, accruals and annual variances) + Risk management and reporting + Dispute resolution / avoidance + Offering commercial strategy advice + Review and recommendation of subcontractor acceptance + Assessment of compensation events + Assessment of monthly payment applications + Commercial audits * Mentoring of junior members of team **Candidate specification** **Essential** * Full Membership of the Royal Institution of Chartered Surveyors (MRICS) * Cost planning skills and experience of delivering ideally within a cost consultancy / multi\-disciplinary environment * Previous experience of contract administration, cost estimates and NEC contracts * Relevant experience working on transportation/highways projects is preferred * Ideally degree qualified * Ability to operate both autonomously and as a part of a larger team * Excellent IT Skills – Word/Excel/PowerPoint * Strong communication skills including writing reports and presenting * Ability to establish and maintain good working relationships with clients, stakeholders, colleagues and the supply chain * Ability to demonstrate a flexible approach to work * Flexibility to travel to client sites and other MM offices Please be advised that offers for this role are conditional upon obtaining the appropriate level of Security Clearance. **We Offer Some Fantastic Benefits Including** * Flexible working * Pension matched up to 7% of base salary * Competitive annual leave * An annual professional institution subscription * Life insurance * Income continuance * Enhanced Paternity leave * Annual company performance bonus scheme – Subject to company performance * Cycle to work scheme * Travel TaxSaver tickets * Commitment to Continuing Professional Development – we are accredited by Engineers Ireland * Mentoring and support for career development and progression **Employee Ownership** * Our employee ownership model means no external investors, just us, creating a culture of shared success. * Our employees have a stake and a voice in our business, giving them a direct connection to our success through our personal and group performance bonuses. * As your career grows, so does your stake, recognising your long\-term impact and contribution. * Your voice matters, with the opportunity to connect directly with senior leadership through formal channels to help shape our future. * For our senior roles you will have a direct title: RETAIL ASSOCIATE
description: TJX Europe At TJX Europe, every day brings new opportunities for growth, exploration, and achievement. You’ll be part of our vibrant team that embraces diversity, fosters collaboration, and prioritises your development. Whether you’re working in our Distribution Centers, Corporate Offices, or Retail Stores—TK Maxx \& Homesense, you’ll find abundant opportunities to learn, thrive, and make an impact. Join us and be part of a place where every day is a chance to make a difference. As proud as we are of our past success, it’s our future that excites us most. title: Regional Environmental Engineer, AWS Environmental
description: **Description** For over 15 years, Amazon Web Services (AWS) has been the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud computing platform. What is cloud computing? Cloud computing is the on\-demand delivery of IT resources over the Internet. In 2019, Amazon announced The Climate Pledge, which includes our commitment to power our operations with 100% renewable energy by 2025, on the path to achieving net zero carbon across our businesses by 2040\. To learn more about AWS, visit https://aws.amazon.com and https://sustainability.aboutamazon.com/environment/the\-cloud. The AWS Environmental team is an expanding and dynamic team that is critical to enabling AWS’s growth around the world, as well as ensuring compliance of AWS’s global operations, including physical data centers, as well as other AWS facilities, customer\-facing enterprises and products. The Environmental team has responsibility for developing and implementing AWS’s global environmental programs. We are seeking a Regional Environmental Engineer to be responsible for AWS environmental compliance in Dublin. The Regional Environmental Engineer will ensure standards and policies support regulatory compliance, and they will provide appropriate environmental guidance to regional operations, design and construction teams. The individual will complete contractual and environmental performance analysis, establish and monitor metrics and guide partner teams in the implementation of Amazon environmental procedures. The Regional Environmental Engineer will work with internal and external partners that include government entities, contractors, consultants, design, construction, operations and other key stakeholders. The Regional Environmental Engineer must have excellent communication skills, strong environmental regulatory knowledge, demonstrated success in program development and project management of complex and technical projects. AWS Infrastructure Services owns the design, planning, delivery, and operation of all AWS global infrastructure. In other words, we’re the people who keep the cloud running. We support all AWS data centers and all of the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that ensure our customers have continual access to the innovation they rely on. We work on the most challenging problems, with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain — and we’re looking for talented people who want to help. You’ll join a diverse team of network engineers, supply chain specialists, safety experts, operations managers, and other vital team members in design and construction. You’ll collaborate with people across AWS to help us deliver the highest standards in environmental compliance while providing seemingly infinite capacity at the lowest possible cost for our customers. And you’ll experience an inclusive culture that welcomes bold ideas and empowers you to own them to completion. Key job responsibilities * Develop and implement comprehensive environmental strategic programs across multiple sites, integrating insights from operations, engineering, and construction teams * Manage environmental compliance by navigating local and national regulations, ensuring adherence to Amazon's rigorous standards * Conduct thorough environmental audits and investigations, creating actionable insights and corrective measures * Build collaborative relationships with governmental agencies, internal stakeholders, and external consultants * Create and track environmental performance metrics to measure, monitor, and forecast organizational impact **About The Team** Diverse Experiences Amazon values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying. Why AWS Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses. Work/Life Balance We value work\-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve in the cloud. Inclusive Team Culture Here at AWS, it’s in our nature to learn and be curious. Our employee\-led affinity groups foster a culture of inclusion that empower us to be proud of our differences. Ongoing events and learning experiences, including our Conversations on Race and Ethnicity (CORE) and AmazeCon (gender diversity) conferences, inspire us to never stop embracing our uniqueness. Mentorship and Career Growth We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge\-sharing, mentorship and other career\-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better\-rounded professional. **Basic Qualifications** * Bachelor's Degree in environmental engineering, environmental science, geology, geography, biology, chemistry or related field with 5\+ years of experience in environmental management or technical advisory roles * Demonstrated knowledge of Irish and EU environmental regulations, with experience in environmental compliance, permitting, waste management, and water discharge requirements * Experience implementing environmental management systems (such as ISO14001\) and conducting environmental impact assessments * Project management experience with proficiency in MS Office applications **Preferred Qualifications** * Knowledge of critical data center equipment * Knowledge of engineering documentation, electrical diagrams and standard operating procedures * Experience in project management in data centers or comparable critical infrastructure * Experience in Data Center Engineering Operations, with a deep understanding of electrical and mechanical data center infrastructure * Experience reading and interpreting construction specifications and drawings for all domains Amazon is an equal opportunities employer. We believe passionately that employing a diverse workforce is central to our success. We make recruiting decisions based on your experience and skills. We value your passion to discover, invent, simplify and build. Protecting your privacy and the security of your data is a longstanding top priority for Amazon. Please consult our Privacy Notice (https://www.amazon.jobs/en/privacy\_page) to know more about how we collect, use and transfer the personal data of our candidates. Amazon is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of protected veteran status, disability, or other legally protected status. Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how\-we\-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner. **Company** \- Amazon Data Services Ireland Limited Job ID: A3180683

title: Software Engineer (Full time)
description: **Company Description** **Nomad AI is Ireland's first AI integration firm, based in Dublin. We help businesses across Ireland, the UK, and Europe leverage AI responsibly — through training, strategy, and custom solutions that deliver real outcomes. We're a Cursor SMB Ambassador, partnered with Skillnet Ireland across 10\+ networks nationwide, and recently placed runner\-up for Innovative Business at the DLR Business Awards. Our focus is making AI accessible, practical, and safe for SMEs and enterprise clients in engineering, construction, and professional services.** **Role Description** **We are hiring a full\-time Software Developer to build AI\-integrated applications for our enterprise clients and contribute to Nomad's own product suite, including Vantage (our analytics platform) and Nomad OS (our custom software platform). You will take full\-stack ownership — from architecture and development through to deployment, monitoring, and iteration. Day\-to\-day you will ship production features, integrate with AI/ML APIs (Claude, OpenAI, Azure AI), and work directly alongside our founders and Forward Deployed Engineers on impactful, real\-world projects. This is a remote\-first role with optional hybrid access to our Dublin office.** **Qualifications** * Strong proficiency in TypeScript, React, and Next.js * Experience with PostgreSQL, Supabase, or an equivalent database * Cloud deployment experience with AWS, Azure, or GCP * REST and GraphQL API design * CI/CD pipelines, Docker, and deployment automation * Hands\-on experience integrating AI/ML APIs (OpenAI, Anthropic, Azure AI) * Solid understanding of authentication, authorisation, and data encryption * Comfortable with Git, code review, and modern testing frameworks * Bachelor's degree in Computer Science or a related field, or equivalent practical experience * Must be based in Ireland or EU timezone

title: Sous Chef

If you thrive in a fast\-paced, creative, quality\-driven environment this is your chance to shape something fresh. **What You'll Do** * Support the Head Chef in daily kitchen operations * Lead the team to deliver consistent, high\-quality dishes * Assist with menu development, specials \& innovation * Maintain top\-tier hygiene, safety \& HACCP standards * Train, motivate, and develop junior chefs * Manage ordering, stock control \& prep efficiency * Uphold a positive, collaborative kitchen culture **What We're Looking For** * Experience as a Sous Chef or strong Junior Sous ready to step up * Passion for food, flavour, and exceptional presentation * Strong leadership and communication skills * Calm, organised, and solutions\-focused under pressure * A commitment to quality, consistency, and guest satisfaction * A team player with a positive attitude and strong work ethic Why work for the group: * Employee Assistance Program * Fun staff days out * % off our sister sites * Superb training * Tips shared * Competitive salary * Free Staff Meals on Duty * Employee Recognition Award * Employee of the month * Bike to work * Strong company culture * Staff referral program * Great opportunities for career progression **About Us** The Wright Group is an award\-winning F\&B hospitality group that builds unique 'out\-of\-the\-box' brands for all of life's celebrations and the moments that matter. title: Specialist, Client Operations II
description: **Job Description** At BNY, our culture allows us to run our company better and enables employees’ growth and success. As a leading global financial services company at the heart of the global financial system, we influence nearly 20% of the world’s investible assets. Every day, our teams harness cutting\-edge AI and breakthrough technologies to collaborate with clients, driving transformative solutions that redefine industries and uplift communities worldwide. Recognized as a top destination for innovators, BNY is where bold ideas meet advanced technology and exceptional talent. Together, we power the future of finance – and this is what is all about. Join us and be part of something extraordinary. We’re seeking a future team member for the role of Associate, Client Operations II to join our Client Service team. This role is located in Dublin. * Day to day oversight of the assigned Collateralised Loan Obligations (“CLOs”) and /or loan portfolios to ensure high Client Experience Levels * Transaction Management: \- Cash \& Trade (Loans, Bonds etc) Management Oversight – ability to engage with Clients to ensure timely execution, Report Review and preparation on CLO and or loan Portfolio’s Performance, Monitoring of Service Level * Collaboration: Continuous dialogue and open communication with Lead Client Service Manager (CSM) and Group Manager on status of work being completed by supporting teams * Participate in Client Meetings and follow up items are closed out * Develop working relationships with Front Office Relationship Managers * Identify and resolve issues while serving as a high\-level escalation point for complex situations and ensure that they are brought to a successful resolution * Employee Development: Key role in training of support team on Client and deal specific requirements * Partner with Lead CSM to identify and streamline business operations. Support the efforts to enhance controls by standardising relevant business processes and consistently managing/monitoring those activities to ensure quality and accuracy. Support key business programs, including business and process improvements which drive greater operational efficiencies and superior client service * Support Lead CSM as an escalation point and communicate effectively with other internal shared services, external investment managers, brokers, and clients. This role sets the directions and tones for the client service administration elements of our business and therefore drives overall client relationships and risk management * Partner Management: proactively monitor and manage the activities performed within our business partners and hold regular meetings and escalate any concerns To be successful in this role, we’re seeking the following: * Corporate Trust or related financial markets/securities industry experience. * Strong Client Management Experience * Ability to review deal governing documents * Experience in Loans (syndicated, bi\-lateral, real estate) and CLOs very desirable * Solid understanding of the product(s) administered by the group and thorough understanding of risk management and control practices. * Proactively monitor and manage the activities performed within our business partners outside of Ireland * Ability to quickly address issues – strong decision making * Financial Services Technical Skills * Effective interpersonal, oral and written communication skills * Excellent analytical, organisational and time management skills with the ability to meet tight deadlines * Proficiency in using Microsoft packages particularly Excel At BNY, our culture speaks for itself, check out the latest BNY news at: BNY Newsroom BNY LinkedIn **Here’s a Few Of Our Recent Awards** * America’s Most Innovative Companies, Fortune, 2025 * World’s Most Admired Companies, Fortune 2025 * “Most Just Companies”, Just Capital and CNBC, 2025 **Our Benefits And Rewards** BNY offers highly competitive compensation, benefits, and wellbeing programs rooted in a strong culture of excellence and our pay\-for\-performance philosophy. We provide access to flexible global resources and tools for your life’s journey. Focus on your health, foster your personal resilience, and reach your financial goals as a valued member of our team, along with generous paid leaves, including paid volunteer time, that can support you and your family through moments that matter. BNY is an Equal Employment Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer \- Underrepresented racial and ethnic groups/Females/Individuals with Disabilities/Protected Veterans.

title: Talent Acquisition Partner - Global SaaS (IDV)
description: **Talent Acquisition Partner – Global SaaS (IDV) Company** **Role Summary:** As a full lifecycle recruitment \& hiring partner within our fast‑growing global SaaS organization in the Identity Verification (IDV) space, you will play a critical role in scaling our high‑performing teams across Product, Technology, Engineering, GTM, and Corporate functions. You will drive full‑cycle recruitment across multiple regions to include the US, EMEA, and APAC, ensuring we attract, assess, and hire exceptional talent aligned with our innovation‑driven culture. You will work closely with hiring managers, HR partners, and business leaders across our offices in th **e US, Ireland, Australia, and Serb** ia, as well as remote talent across 10\+ countries. This role requires a proactive, strategic, and relationship‑driven recruiter who thrives in global, fast‑paced environments **.** This is a 12\-month fixed term contract with opportunity for extension or conversion. Hybrid work schedule with 3 days on\-site and 2 days remote. **Key Responsibilities:** **Full‑Cycle Recruitment** Manage the end‑to‑end hiring process for a diverse range of roles, with a heavy focus on **:** * **Engineering** (Backend, Frontend, Full‑stack, Mobile) * **DevOps / SRE / Cloud Infrastructure** * **Tech Development / Research** * **Product Management \& UX** * **GTM functions** (Sales, Marketing, Customer Success) * **Corporate roles** across HR, Finance, Legal, IT and Operations Own job scoping, talent market insights, sourcing strategy, interview management, offer negotiation, and pre‑boarding. **Sourcing \& Talent Pipelining** * Build strong pipelines through proactive sourcing, networking, research, and partnerships, * Leverage LinkedIn, GitHub, Stack Overflow, niche technical communities, and global/regional talent platforms. * Develop and maintain pools of high‑value candidates in key technical domains. **Hiring Manager Partnership** * Act as a consultative recruiting partner to Product, Engineering, and Tech leadership * Provide market insights, compensation benchmarking, and candidate profile calibration. * Guide hiring managers on interview practices, evaluation standards, and best‑in‑class recruitment approaches. **Global Recruitment Operations** * Navigate recruiting across the US, Ireland, Australia, Serbia, and additional remote countries with sensitivity to local employment practices. * Partner closely with: Global Head of HR (US), HR Manager for EMEA \& APAC (Ireland), Senior HR Business Partner (Serbia). * Ensure consistent and compliant hiring practices globally. **Candidate Experience** * Craft a positive, transparent, and inclusive candidate experience across time zones and regions. * Act as the brand ambassador for our company mission, culture, and values. **Data‑Driven Recruiting** * Track and report hiring metrics (time to fill, pipeline analytics, sourcing effectiveness, DEI indicators). * Use data to optimize processes and align hiring strategies with business pr **iorities.** **Process \& Employer Brand Development** * Contribute to process improvements, interview training programs, and ATS optimization. * Support employer branding initiatives, including content creation, event participation, and storytelling aligned with our IDV mission and tech culture. **Qualifications \& Experience** * 4–7\+ years of full‑cycle recruiting experience, with at least **3 years in technical recruitment for high‑growth SaaS or tech‑driven** companies. * Demonstrated experience hiring across global markets (US, EMEA, APAC preferred). * Proven success recruiting for complex technical roles (engineering, cloud infrastructure, DevOps, research, or product).Exceptional sourcing skills with creative approaches to global talent markets. * Strong influencing and partnership capabilities with senior stakeholders. * Comfort working across time zones and collaborating with a distributed HR team. * Knowledge of applicant tracking systems and recruitment analytics. * Ability to operate independently, identify solutions, and manage competing priorities in a dynamic, scaling environment. * Passion for tech, innovation, and the identity verification/AI/ML ecosystem is a plus. **What Success Looks Like:** * High‑quality hires made efficiently for critical Product \& Technology as well as other department roles. * Strong hiring manager satisfaction and credible recruiting partnerships. * Robust candidate pipelines established across key technical disciplines. * Improved recruiting process scalability across global offices. * Consistent, positive candidate experiences that strengthen employer brand. * Data‑driven insights influencing workforce planning and hiring strategies. ***Daon is an Equal Opportunity Employer and is committed to fostering an inclusive and diverse workplace. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, national origin, age, disability, or any other legally protected status.***

title: Tax Technology & Transformation - Associate
description: **Line of Service** Tax **Industry/Sector** Not Applicable **Specialism** General Tax Consulting **Management Level** Associate **Job Description \& Summary** About us: PwC Ireland’s Tax Technology \& Transformation team is part of Ireland’s leading Tax practice. We are a dynamic Partner led team with diverse Tax, Finance and IT backgrounds. Our Tax Technology \& Transformation team works with market leading clients in a broad range of industry sectors to maximise operational effectiveness within their tax functions by leveraging best in class approaches and solutions. We help clients design and execute the right tax technology strategies to manage tax risks, increase efficiency and take advantage of the opportunities enabled by technology. The tax landscape is changing and we are continuously expanding due to our clients’ growing demand to leverage more efficient processes and technology to support the tax function as the volume and complexity of global tax compliance and regulatory requirements continues to increase. **What we do:** Our work is increasingly varied and involves supporting organisations with all aspects of tax technology and transformation across all tax heads including: * The development of long term tax function technology strategies and alignment of same with wider business operating model objectives; * The design, build and implementation of tax solutions solving tax problems leveraging best\-in\-class artificial intelligence, robotics, data extraction, transformation and analytics functionality; * The implementation of our market leading tax compliance and reporting solutions as well as tax workflow management and PwC hosted Software as a Service (SaaS) solutions; * Working with PwC alliance partners to optimise core systems to support the delivery of data in a fit\-for\-purpose for tax process execution. **What does this role offer?** The changing tax landscape driven by a significant shift to technology initiated challenges by tax authorities, presents an opportunity for tax functions to respond with a technology enabled response. These developments afford our team regular client interactions and the opportunity to support in understanding the tax challenges presented along with the design and implementation of solutions to help them add value in their organisations. As a member of the Tax Technology and Transformation team, you will work as part of a market leading team of problem solvers with extensive tax and technology consulting experience and a diverse blend of skill sets. You will work on a varied portfolio of tax projects with our key clients and develop a broad range of technology and tax consulting skill sets. **About you – Key Qualifications/Skills \& Experience:** * Part ACA / ITI qualified (or equivalent) preferable * 3 years finance or tax transformation project management experience in industry or practice * Proven experience designing and implementing tax / finance transformation automation solutions * Certification or functional experience with technology solutions such as the MS Power Platform, Python, SQL, Alteryx etc ***Note:** This role is not open for relocation or work\-permit sponsorship.* **Create your future with us.** The world of work has changed and we are changing with it! At PwC, we believe that challenges are better solved together. A career with us means joining a purpose led and diverse, global community where you will do meaningful work with our amazing clients. Our culture is unique and what sets us apart. Our people really are at the very heart of our business. So, when you join us, you will be empowered to work flexibly in a way that works for you, your clients and the teams around you, supporting your wellbeing. We work tirelessly so that you can unlock your full potential and give you everything you need to take your career where you want it to go. We appreciate what you bring to our culture,our team and our clients and we recognise and celebrate you for it. Our tech\-powered and global capabilities come together so that you can make an impact not only to clients and our teams but on the world around us too. **PwC Perks** Of course, we also offer all the perks you would expect from a leading employer for every stage of your journey with us. From everyday flexibility to working from abroad, learning and development or reward and recognition. We have best in class inclusive and family friendly perks and policies that meet you where you are\- in your life and in your career. **Inclusion Statement** PwC is proud to be an equal opportunities employer and we believe passionately in employing a diverse workforce so much that we have signed up for the Business in the Community Elevate Pledge. We recognise that confidence and the imposter syndrome can sometimes hold you back from applying for your dream job. Whatever your path is or however you identify, please don’t hesitate to apply \- we would love to hear from you! You can learn more about how we are driving a culture of belonging at PwC, and explore our range of inclusive programmes, initiatives, employee resource groups and more, by visiting www.pwc.ie We will ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform crucial job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. **Required Skills** **Optional Skills** Accepting Feedback, Active Listening, Communication, Domestic Restructuring (Taxes), Emotional Regulation, Empathy, Inclusion, Income Tax Provisions, Intellectual Curiosity, Optimism, Partnership Tax, Retail and Consumer (Taxes), Tax Credit, Taxes, Tax Incentives, Tax Legislation, Tax Modeling, Tax Policy, Tax Provisions, Tax Restructuring, Tax Structures, Teamwork, Technical Writing, Well Being **Desired Languages** *(If blank, desired languages not specified)* **Travel Requirements** Not Specified **Available for Work Visa Sponsorship?** Yes **Government Clearance Required?** No **Job Posting End Date**

title: Test Automation Engineer
description: Do you want to join an exciting, growing team that wants to hear your ideas? A team that wants you to be up to date on the latest technologies. Working in BearingPoint you will be part of a team that is passionate about building innovative, high\-quality software for its clients. You will be working in agile teams with all the support you need. BearingPoint is growing quickly, and we are currently hiring junior automation testers to join our QA team. **What skills and experience should you have?** * Passionate about technology, testing and delivering top quality software products. * At least 5 years’ experience in test automation. * Practical experience of test automation including tooling selection and test framework design. * Hands\-on technical experience of building both API and UI test automation frameworks in a range of languages and technologies such as jUnit, Selenium, Serenity BDD (Cucumber, Rest Assured) Robot Framework and JMeter. * Implementation, maintenance and execution of automated test suites to ensure thorough testing of requirements in a fast\-paced Agile environment with an emphasis on BDD and BDD automated tools. * Disciplined reporting, tracking, and resolution of product defects. Implementation of reporting technologies, integrated with automated test runs. * Implementation and management of automated test scripts in CI/CD pipelines. * Experience carrying out code reviews and adhering to best practice guidelines. * Clear understanding of defect management lifecycle. * Hands on knowledge of SQL. * Knowledge of scripting languages (Shell, Perl, Python etc.). * Excellent verbal and written communication skills who excels when working in a multi\-disciplinary agile team. **Why you will enjoy working at BearingPoint Ireland?** We work in an open and collaborative way, where new ideas are always welcome. BearingPoint is an environment where potential and high performance is recognised and rewarded. **What we offer:** You will receive a competitive salary where you are rewarded for both your individual performance and company performance. You will also receive a best\-in\-class benefits package including holidays, private health insurance, pension contributions, gym/club subscription, wellness programmes, mobile phone and much more. In addition, we offer: **Hybrid working –** where each team has autonomy to determine their in\-person and remote working schedule. **Continuous learning –** we understand that it is imperative to invest in our people’s development and we provide external training at prestigious universities such as Oxford and Yale **Social events –** our people perform best when they have a good work life balance, monthly events for all to attend **Diversity** – we are committed to having a diverse workforce where each individual feels they belong and can thrive in a work environment with a strong sense of community **Mentoring \& coaching –** every employee has a development manager to help them grow their career with feedback via continuous conversations **Giving Back –** you will have an opportunity to join our many giving back initiatives including our commitment to sustainability. Our Emissions Calculator gathers accurate emissions data and enables our clients to understand their footprint and track progress on emission reduction and pave the way for a better future. **Together, we are more than business.** To see more from our people with their experiences of BearingPoint please visit our careers website www.bearingpoint.com **About BearingPoint** BearingPoint is an independent management and technology consultancy with European roots and a global reach. The company operates in four units: Consulting, Solutions, Business Services, and Ventures. Consulting covers the advisory business; Solutions provides the tools for successful digital transformation, advanced analytics and regulatory requirements; Business Services provides managed services beyond SaaS; Ventures drives the financing and development of start\-ups. BearingPoint’s clients include many of the world’s leading companies and organizations. The firm has a global consulting network with more than 10,000 people and supports clients in over 75 countries, engaging with them to achieve measurable and sustainable success. BearingPoint is an equal opportunities employer. Please click here to view our privacy policy. **NO AGENCIES** BearingPoint does not accept unsolicited cvs from 3rd party recruitment agencies without a written agreement in place for placements. BearingPoint is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited cvs and no fee will be paid should the candidate be hired by BearingPoint.

title: Test Engineer
description: **Company Description** Version 1 has celebrated over 28 years in Technology Services and continues to be trusted by global brands to deliver solutions that drive customer success. Our expertise enables our customers to navigate the rapidly changing Digital\-First world we live in. We foster strong partnerships with leading technology giants including Microsoft, AWS, Oracle, Red Hat, OutSystems, Snowflake, ensuring that our customers are provided with the highest quality solutions and services. We’re An Award\-winning Employer Reflecting How Our Employees Are At The Very Heart Of Version 1 And What We Do * UK \& Ireland's premier AWS, Microsoft \& Oracle partner * 3300\+ strong, €350/£300m revenue business * 10\+ years as a Great Place to Work in Ireland \& UK * Best Workplace for Women in the UK \& Ireland by GPTW * Best Workplace for Wellbeing in the UK by GPTW We’re a core values driven company, we hire people who share our values, and we reward those who display and foster them, it’s deeply embedded within our DNA. Invest in us and we’ll invest in you! **Job Description** You may have been working as a Software Engineer in Test, Test Engineer or Test Analyst who is confident in their ability performing manual test but also enjoy conducting automation testing. You'll be part of an agile team helping our clients achieve their business transformation goals by identifying requirements, developing solutions and providing ongoing guidance and advice in test. **Key Responsibilities** * Work closely with cross\-functional teams to ensure quality standards are met across projects. * Validate and analyse large volumes of data using SQL. * Develop and maintain reports and dashboards using tools such as SSRS and Power BI. * Support test management processes, leveraging tools like QMetry. * Contribute to automation testing initiatives, with a focus on API testing (experience with Karate is highly desirable). * Participate in knowledge\-sharing activities and foster collaboration across regions and functions. **Qualifications** * Domain Knowledge: Strong understanding of Finance and Insurance comfortable with financial terminology used in business discussions. * Technical Expertise: + Proficiency in SQL for data validation and analysis. + Experience with reporting and data visualisation tools (SSRS, Power BI). * Testing Skills: + Familiarity with test management tools (e.g., QMetry). + Automation testing experience, particularly in API testing (Karate preferred). * Soft Skills: + Clear and proactive communicator. + Strong team player with the ability to work effectively across diverse teams and regions. + Adaptable and eager to learn in a collaborative, inclusive environment. **Work Arrangement** * Office Attendance: This role requires working from the office 4 days per week, Dublin based **Additional Information** **Why Version 1?** At Version 1, we believe in providing our employees with a comprehensive benefits package that prioritises their wellbeing, professional growth, and financial stability. * Share in our success with our Quarterly Performance\-Related Profit Share Scheme, where employees collectively benefit from a share of our company's profits. * Strong Career Progression \& mentorship coaching through our Strength in Balance \& Leadership schemes with a dedicated quarterly Pathways Career Development programme. * Flexible/remote working, Version 1 is tremendously understanding of life events and people’s individual circumstances and offer flexibility to help achieve a healthy work life balance. * Financial Wellbeing initiatives including; Pension, Private Healthcare Cover, Life Assurance, Financial advice and an Employee Discount scheme. * Employee Wellbeing schemes including Gym Discounts, Bike to Work, Fitness classes, Mindfulness Workshops, Employee Assistance Programme and much more. Generous holiday allowance, enhanced maternity/paternity leave, marriage/civil partnership leave and special leave policies. * Educational assistance, incentivised certifications, and accreditations, including AWS, Microsoft, Oracle, and Red Hat. * Reward schemes including Version 1’s Annual Excellence Awards \& ‘Call\-Out’ platform. * Environment, Social and Community First initiatives allow you to get involved in local fundraising and development opportunities as part of fostering our diversity, inclusion and belonging schemes. And many more exciting benefits… drop us a note to find out more.

title: Treasury Analyst (EMEA)
description: SpaceX was founded under the belief that a future where humanity is out exploring the stars is fundamentally more exciting than one where we are not. Today SpaceX is actively developing the technologies to make this possible, with the ultimate goal of enabling human life on Mars. **TREASURY ANALYST (EMEA)** SpaceX is looking for a Treasury Analyst to join our team to ensure that our treasury capabilities meet the increasing needs of our rapidly growing global Starlink business. This position will support various aspects of treasury operations, including new\-country expansions, cash management, FX strategy and execution, fixed\-income investments, payments, and systems. **Responsibilities** * Build and maintain the company’s expanding worldwide banking network * Research and resolve inquiries regarding global cash activities * Support in the execution of foreign exchange trades required to fund SpaceX's global business * Perform risk analyses related to foreign exchange and other risks * Work with banking partners and FX liquidity providers to explore new systems and methodologies for executing trades in restricted currencies * Investigate opportunities to enhance and standardize processes related to FX trading/reporting and fixed income investment trading/reporting * Execute best practices/controls across all treasury functions * Perform reconciliations and build forecasting mechanisms as needed * Work closely with the global treasury team in the US, Singapore and Ireland to design and build risk monitoring frameworks, systems, and reporting dashboards that present risks and mitigation plans or alternatives **Basic Qualifications** * Bachelor's degree in finance, accounting, economics, business or other business discipline * 2\+ years of corporate treasury or experience in a multinational corporate treasury function or global bank **Preferred Skills And Experience** * Excellent communication (written and verbal), analytical and interpersonal skills * Ability to work independently and handle multiple tasks under tight deadlines * Comfortable with Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint * Experience with payments/cash transfers and related procedures, controls and systems * Experience with corporate treasury FX trading platforms such as FXAll, 360T, and Bloomberg FXGO * Familiarity with treasury management systems (e.g. Kyriba, SAP, FIS) and online banking platforms **Additional Requirements** * This position is based in Dublin and requires being onsite \- remote work is not considered * Must be available to work extended hours and weekends as needed SpaceX is an Equal Opportunity Employer; employment with SpaceX is governed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications and will not be influenced in any manner by race, color, religion, gender, national origin/ethnicity, veteran status, disability status, age, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, mental or physical disability or any other legally protected status.

title: Validation Specialist
description: Life changing therapies. Global impact. Bridge to thousands of biopharma companies and their patients. **We are PCI.** Our investment is in People who make an impact, drive progress and create a better tomorrow. Our strategy includes building teams across our global network to pioneer and shape the future of PCI. We are looking for a **Validation Specialist** (Permanent role) to join our team in Stamullen, County Meath. This is a Monday to Friday position, 100% on\-site. **Summary Of Role** The role of the Validation Specialist is to implement and execute tasks relating to Process, Facility and Equipment Development at PCI Pharma Services sites. **Areas Of Validation Included Are** Process Validation (PV) Cleaning Validation (CV) Computer Systems Validation (CSV) **Key Responsibilities** * Ensure safe work practices are being followed at all times, report any near misses immediately to the EHS Department * Ensuring all validation activities on site relating to Facility, Equipment and Processes meet the expectations of clients but more specifically meet regulatory requirements (HPRA/ FDA). * Comply with Site Validation Master Plan. * Generate validation protocols for new and existing computer systems, facilities, equipment and processes and review/approve all reports. Also to generate and maintain documentation for cleaning validation performed at PCI sites in Ireland. * Execute validation activities, with minimal supervision, in conjunction with Technical, Production and Engineering functions. * Perform sampling in support of validation studies, including AQL, and cleaning validation. * Manage tooling validations and associated documentation. * Ensure the strict implementation of validation procedures plus the timely and accurate completion of all validation documentation. * Support complaint investigations, deviation investigations and change controls as required. * To undertake such tasks and to manage specific or ad hoc projects as and when required to meet business needs and within the scope of the job\-holder’s capabilities. * Identify and support opportunities for improving processes and or procedures. * Perform Periodic reviews and revalidations on a scheduled basis. * Work with Validation team to ensure a robust and compliant validation system is in place. * Ensure validation being performed is in line with industry standards and latest regulatory guidelines. * Carry out various other additional tasks that may be required from time to time in accordance with the overall goal of the company. **Essential** * Bachelor’s or associate degree or Diploma / Certificate in Quality / Validation. May substitute experience in lieu of educational requirements. * Minimum 2 years’ experience working in Manufacturing or Quality. * Demonstrate interest in Validation. * Capability to follow clear goals, communicate clearly and ensure progress to completion meeting predefined targets. * Ability to manage multiple tasks and set priorities. * Problem Solving and Troubleshooting skills. The successful candidate should display a dynamic and proactive approach to manage problems. * Self\-motivated with a results driven approach. * Flexibility to work across different shifts on request in line with business needs. * Adaptable and ability to work collaboratively. * Proficient in Microsoft Office products (incl. Excel, PowerPoint, Word, etc.). * Experience in a GMP or Regulated Environment. **Desirable** * Experience in Lean Manufacturing, including experience in Lean tools to develop continuous improvements. Join us and be part of building the bridge between life changing therapies and patients. Let’s talk future **Equal Employment Opportunity (EEO) Statement** *PCI Pharma Services is an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer. We do not unlawfully discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, age, sex, creed, national origin, ancestry, citizenship status, marital or domestic or civil union status, familial status, affectional or sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, genetics, disability, military eligibility or veteran status, or any other protected status.* *At PCI, Equity and Inclusion are at the core of our company’s purpose: Together, delivering life\-changing therapies. We are committed to cultivating an inclusive workplace by holding ourselves accountable to the highest standards of understanding, fairness, respect, and equal opportunity – at every level. We envision a PCI community where everyone can belong and grow, and we strive to bring this vision to reality by continuously and intentionally assessing our people practices, policies and programs, marketing approach, and workplace culture.*

title: Valuation Surveyors (All levels)
description: **Who Are We** Founded in 1896, Knight Frank was registered as Knight Frank LLP a Limited Liability Partnership, on 3 November 2003, registered in England \& Wales at 55 Baker Street, London, W1U 8AN with the registered number of OC305934\. Headquartered in London, UK, Knight Frank is one of the world's leading independent real estate consultancies. We work responsibly in partnership to enhance people's lives and environments ***in over 50 Markets, 600\+ Offices, 20,000\+ People, 1 Global Network.*** At the heart of all we do are our Clients. A relationship built on trust is vital. As trusted partners in property, we act with integrity and care, understanding our clients’ unique needs and consistently thinking about the bigger picture to provide personalised, clear and considered advice across all areas of property. Through our deep understanding of key markets and sectors – both emerging and established – we are dedicated to meeting and exceeding their property goals. A true partnership is a balanced one, and our People are passionate about, and committed to, making our business inclusive and diverse. We give every individual the space and opportunity to perform at their best and be recognised for the immense value they bring. We empower everyone to have autonomy in their role and encourage them to use their voice in how we can make a positive impact as a firm and shape the future of real estate, the built environment and the impact on the communities we serve. Our collaborative and engaged teams provide excellent and dedicated client service. In our workplace, opinions are respected, everyone is invited to contribute to the success of our business and innovation, and new ideas are celebrated. In 2021 we celebrated our 125th anniversary and set out Our Desired Future, cemented in three pillars: People Potential, Client Centricity and always Creating our Future, together. Learn more about what sets us apart here. **About The Role** We are growing our market‑leading Valuation \& Advisory division and are seeking ambitious Chartered Valuation Surveyors at all levels — from graduate surveyor/newly qualified surveyors ready to build their expertise, to senior leaders seeking a platform to develop and mentor teams and drive client relationships. Apply now, if you want to work with a firm that values technical excellence, supports professional growth, and gives you exposure to some of Ireland’s most significant assets and clients. **Key Responsibilities** ***Newly Qualified Surveyors/Graduates*** * Assist in preparing high\-quality RICS Red Book valuation and advisory reports for banks, funds, developers, and corporate clients * Conduct inspections, market research, and comparable analysis * Support senior colleagues on complex instructions * Build strong client relationships and develop commercial awareness * Contribute to pitches, proposals, and team initiatives ***Senior up to Director\-Level Surveyors*** * Lead valuation instructions and manage key client relationships * Oversee and review valuation reports to ensure accuracy and compliance * Engage in Landlord \& Tenant matters, including rent reviews and lease renewals. * Contribute to the periodic review of team processes and procedures. * Mentor and support junior surveyors * Provide expert advice on complex assets, portfolios, and development appraisals * Contribute to shared knowledge within the team and the wider group. * Generate and win new business through networking and client engagement. **Requirements** * MRICS/MSCSI \- VRS Registered Valuer * Strong Technical Knowledge of valuation methodologies including Discounted Cash Flows. * Knowledge of Argus Enterprise and Developer Valuation packages. * Professional presentation with good customer service and communication skills required. * Excellent written and verbal communication skills \& attention to detail is essential. * Strong time management and organisational skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities. * Team player with strong interpersonal skills Competitive salary Please note: this is a Direct Search led by Knight Frank. Applications from recruitment agencies will not be accepted nor will fees be paid for unsolicited CVs, even if provided by PSL agencies.

title: Veterinary Surgeon – Small Animal (GP Focus)
description: **Vetcare Athy, Co. Kildare Full\-time, Fixed Term Contract (Part\-time considered) Salary up to €70,000 DOE (FTE) Start date from May 2026 (flexible)** **A Solid GP Role in a Well\-Run, Supportive Practice** Vetcare Athy is seeking a confident, well\-rounded small animal Veterinary Surgeon to join the team on a maternity cover contract. This role is ideal for an experienced GP vet who enjoys the rhythm of first opinion practice, is comfortable managing a varied caseload, and takes pride in delivering consistent, high\-quality care. You’ll be joining a busy but well\-organised clinic where the focus is on doing the fundamentals well. This is a role for someone who values good clinical standards, enjoys building client relationships, and is confident across routine consults, surgery and day\-to\-day case management. **The Role – Confident First Opinion Practice** This is a full\-time position working 42 hours per week, Monday to Friday from 9:00am to 6:00pm, with a 1 in 4 Saturday rota from 9:00am to 3:00pm. You’ll be managing a broad and steady caseload, including consultations, routine surgeries, dentistry and medical work\-ups. The role will suit someone who is comfortable with the pace of general practice and capable of working independently when needed, including periods of sole charge. There are no out\-of\-hours requirements, no Sundays and no bank holiday work, giving you a predictable schedule and a good balance outside of work. **A Practice That Supports Good Clinical Work** Vetcare Athy is the flagship clinic within the Vetcare group, offering a well\-equipped and professionally run environment. While the focus remains on first opinion care, the practice also supports more complex case work\-ups and procedures, giving you the option to expand your clinical exposure if that’s of interest. Facilities include digital and dental X\-ray, ultrasound and a fully equipped in\-house laboratory, allowing you to work cases to a high standard. The team is experienced, approachable and collaborative, creating an environment where you can focus on doing your job well without unnecessary pressure. **Flexible Options to Suit You** While this is advertised as a full\-time position, we are very open to hearing from experienced vets seeking part\-time or alternative working patterns. If you’re looking for something that better fits around other commitments, let us know what works for you and we’ll do our best to accommodate where possible. **Who This Role Will Suit** This position is best suited to an experienced Veterinary Surgeon who is confident in routine small animal practice. You’ll be comfortable with consultations, diagnostics, routine soft tissue surgery and dentistry, and happy managing cases from start to finish. You’ll also be someone who works well within a team, communicates clearly, and takes a practical, steady approach to clinical work. This is not a role for a new graduate, but it is ideal for a GP vet who enjoys the variety and consistency of first opinion practice and wants to work in a well\-supported setting. **Part of a Wider, Supportive Network** As part of the Vetcare group and IVC Evidensia, you’ll have access to a wider clinical network, resources and potential future opportunities. While this is a fixed\-term contract, it can be a great way to build relationships within the group and explore longer\-term options if they arise. **A Package That Reflects Your Experience** Alongside a competitive salary, you’ll receive a comprehensive benefits package designed to support your wellbeing and professional development. This includes generous annual leave, paid CPD, professional memberships, wellbeing supports, pension contributions and a range of additional benefits. **A Straightforward, High\-Quality GP Role** If you’re looking for a role where you can step in, feel confident in your work, and be part of a supportive, well\-run practice, this could be an excellent fit. We’d love to hear from you. €0 \- €70,000 a year Up to €70,000 DOE We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.
